Headline: Subway trains rerouted to elevated tracks
ShortDescription: Red Line subway trains will be rerouted to the elevated tracks between Cermak-Chinatown and Fullerton in both directions.

FullDescription.#cdata-section:
Red Line subway trains will be rerouted to the elevated tracks between Cermak-Chinatown and Fullerton in both directions to allow for subway maintenance.

Trains will not stop at subway stations (see map below).

For fastest travel times, walk to the nearest ‘L’ station served by rerouted trains or use the free bus shuttle service offered between Fullerton and Clark/Lake in both directions.

Allow extra travel time.

*Entry/exit at Merchandise Mart station is via the Kinzie auxiliary entrance stairways, and requires a farecard (no vending machines), during hours when the building is closed. To make step-free transfers to Red Line trains during this time, consider shuttle buses to connect with Clark/Lake or Fullerton.

Headline: Green Line Delays
ShortDescription: Green Line trains are running w/residual delays and possible congestion following signal problems near Ashland station. Service is resuming, allow extra time.

FullDescription.#cdata-section:
Green Line trains are running w/residual delays and possible congestion following signal problems near Ashland station.

Some trains and platforms may be busier than usual as crews work to restore normal service.

Trains are moving and normal service is being restored.

Allow extra travel time.

Headline: Orange Line Service Running with Delays
ShortDescription: Orange Line trains are running with delays due to extreme weather.

FullDescription.#cdata-section:
Orange Line trains are running with delays due to extreme weather. 

At this time consider other service alternatives that may serve your trip, such as nearby bus routes or using buses to other rail lines.

Personnel are working to restore normal service. Allow extra travel time.

Headline: Service Between 54th/Cermak and Polk, then Racine (Blue Line) Stations, Only
ShortDescription: Pink Line trains operate from 54th to Polk only, then connect w/Blue Line at Racine for continuing service, due to signal outages near Ashland station.

FullDescription.#cdata-section:
Pink Line service has been temporarily suspended between Polk and the Loop due to signal outages near Ashland station.

As a result, Pink Line trains are running as follows:

  • Between 54th/Cermak and Polk, and then are diverted to the Racine station on the Blue Line for direct connections at Racine to continuing Blue Line train service to/from downtown.

Using connecting Blue Line service:

  • For service to downtown from Pink Line stations between 54th/Cermak and Polk, take a Pink Line train toward the Loop and stay on board until the train reaches Racine on the Blue Line. Transfer to an O'Hare-bound Blue Line train from the same platform for service into downtown.

  • For service from downtown to Pink Line stations between Polk and 54th/Cermak, take a Blue Line train toward Forest Park to Racine. Stay on the same platform side and wait for a 54th/Cermak-bound Pink Line train.

Other possible alternatives may include, depending on your origin and destination: 

  • #9 Ashland
  • #21 Cermak
  • #49 Western
  • #50 Damen
  • #52 Kedzie/California
  • #53 Pulaski
  • #54 Cicero
  • #60 Blue Island-26th
  • #82 Kimball-Homan
  • #94 South California
  • #157 Streeterville-Taylor buses

...including to/from and in combination with other 'L' services such as the Blue or Green lines.

Note that if you take a bus to/from the Pink Line, you may also be able to connect to/from the Blue Line's Forest Park branch or Green Line along Lake Street directly via that same bus route.

See station personnel for further assistance and pay attention to announcements on trains and at stations for service information.

We are working to restore service as quickly as possible. Allow extra travel time.

Headline: Brown Line Service Running with Delays
ShortDescription: Brown Line trains are running with significant delays due to earlier train traffic/congestion near Kimball.

FullDescription.#cdata-section:
Brown Line trains are running with significant delays due to earlier train/traffic congestion near Kimball.

At this time, also consider other service alternatives that may serve your trip, such as nearby bus routes, or using buses to other rail lines.

Personnel are working to restore normal service. Allow extra travel time.

Headline: #126 Temporary Reroute at Jackson/Springfield
ShortDescription: #126 Jackson buses temporarily rerouted due to, a fallen tree near Jackson/Springfield.

FullDescription.#cdata-section:
#126 Jackson buses temporarily rerouted due to, a fallen tree near Jackson/Springfield.

#126 Jackson will reroute in both directions via Jackson, Laramie, Madison and Cicero over the normal route.

Allow extra travel time.

Headline: #126 Jackson buses Temporary Reroute at Jackson/Lavergne

FullDescription.#cdata-section:
#126 Jackson buses temporarily rerouted due to, street blockage at Jackson/Lavergne.

#126 Jackson will reroute in both directions via Pulaski, Madison, Hamlin and Jackson over the normal route.

Headline: #82 Kimball-Homan Temporary Reroute near Kimball/Armitage
ShortDescription: #82 Kimball-Homan SB buses are temporarily rerouted via: Kimball, Kedzie, North Ave and Homan due to street blockage near Kimball/Armitage.

FullDescription.#cdata-section:
#82 Kimball-Homan Southbound buses are temporarily rerouted via: Kimball, Kedzie, North Ave and Homan due to street blockage near Kimball/Armitage.

Northbound buses are not affected.

Allow extra travel time.
